concept: Corner detection threshold for edge/corner fidelity
source_excerpt: alphamax     default 1.0, logo 0.8, text 1.2   (range 0-1.3334) corner sharpness ... alphamax (corner sharpness) -> corner_threshold (higher potrace alphamax = smoother/rounder -> lower vtracer corner_threshold)
implication: Edge/corner fidelity hinges on whether the tracer preserves sharp corners or rounds them. For hard-edged art (text, geometric logos) corners must stay crisp to keep edge_iou high; over-smoothing rounds corners and blurs the edge map. This is the same alphamax knob the curve agent owns, but here it is framed as protecting corner/edge topology rather than curve smoothness.

param_mapping.rationale: For edge/corner FIDELITY on angular art, bias corner_threshold higher (keep more corners crisp; analogue of potrace text preset alphamax 1.2). This is the opposite bias from the curve agent's smoothing goal, which is exactly why it is a distinct isolated factor: the same param optimizes different metrics (edge_iou here vs ssim-on-curves there) depending on artwork class.

hypothesis: Raising corner_threshold on angular/geometric/text art preserves sharp corners and raises edge_iou, whereas the curve agent's lower setting would round them; the optimal value is artwork-class dependent, so edge_iou and curve-ssim must be traded off explicitly.
